LAW AND MOTION 301, MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLT CONTINUED FROM DEC-21-2010 TO LAW AND MOTION AT JAN-27-2011, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 301 PER AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES.

LAW AND MOTION 301, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF TRAVIS KNAPP-PRASEK'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE SEPARATE CLAIM AGAINST DIFFERENT PARTIES ARISING FROM DIFFERENT CONDUCT AFTER THE ACCIDENT IS BETTER LEFT TO A SEPARATE LAWSUIT. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. TRIAL CONCLUDED. IN FIVE (5) DAYS; JURY DELIBERATIONS CONTINUE; THE JURY DELIVERS A VERDICT IN FAVOR OF THE DEFENDANT; THE COURT THANKS AND DISCHARGES THE TWELVE JURORS AND ALTERNATE JURORS; COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED JUDGMENT FOR THE COURT'S SIGNATURE WITHIN TWENTY-FOUR HOURS; ALL EXHIBITS ORDERED RETURNED TO COUNSEL BY THE COURT. TRIAL IS CONCLUDED. JUDGE: HON. LINDA H. COLFAX; CLERK MARJORIE L. MANZELLA; REPORTER: TEANNA L.WARD, CSR #11918 (DEPT. 613).
